title: D2Game — iOS Grid RPG
problem: Build a mobile game combining Pokemon-style 50×50 grid exploration, Diablo 2 loot philosophy (even normal items can be exciting via sockets/runewords), and roguelite floor progression. No existing iOS game nails all three simultaneously in a pick-up-and-play format.
analysis: Core loop: move on grid → trigger encounter → fast combat gate (<5s) → loot resolves → inventory puzzle → find exit → next floor. Priority hierarchy: Map & Exploration > Encounters > Loot System > Inventory Management >> Combat. Combat is a transaction, not a feature. The fun is in the hunt, the drop, and the Tetris inventory puzzle. Endgame players hunt rare encounters and rare item combinations, not specific fights.
implementation:
Expo (React Native) + TypeScript. Engine layer (src/engine/) is pure TS — zero React, 100% bun-testable. Rendering: plain React Native for all UI (combat, inventory, menus, grid). State: Zustand slices (gridStore, combatStore, inventoryStore, gameStore, settingsStore). Persistence: @react-native-async-storage/async-storage via src/services/persistence.ts (saveGame/scheduleSave/loadGame). Seeded PRNG (mulberry32) ensures every run is reproducible by seed. Sim scripts (sim/) allow headless floor/loot/combat simulation without a device. Class system: 3 classes (Warrior/Rogue/Sorcerer) each with unique stats and 4–5 skills, balanced via Monte Carlo sim. Shared stash persists across deaths in gameStore. Town screen provides character overview + stash management.

Current UX state (verified 2026-04-01):
- HUD: stamina bar hidden when ST > 30%; compact badge + bar shown when ST ≤ 30%. Reduces HUD clutter during normal play.
- GridScreen zoom: persisted to settingsStore.zoomLevel (0|1|2) so preferred zoom survives app restarts.
- Zoom lock toggle: when active, single-finger swipe adjusts zoom instead of moving the character. Zoom-lock button adjacent to zoom in/out.
- Ghost Echo ambient hint: shown once in GridScreen (after 2s delay) when graveyard has items and no echo is active. markGhostEchoHintSeen() ensures it never repeats.
- Combat RNG: fully deterministic — seed ^ (floor * 0x9e3779b9) ^ (roundNumber * 0x6b429177) with unique offsets for attacks, skills, and loot.
- Ascension: ASCEND button on victory screen (when graveyard non-empty) starts a new run with pre-loaded Ghost Echo and increments careerStats.ascensions.
- Pact system: 4 voluntary run curses (blood/suffering/frailty/exhaustion), each with a matching reward. Selected pre-run on ClassSelectScreen, persists across runs. All four wired into combat damage, loot generation, XP, monster spawns, and stamina init.
- Named Variants: 8 elite enemy archetypes with fixed affix combos (e.g. Void Wraith = teleporting+cursed). 20% spawn chance for elite+ tiers from floor 4+. Reuses existing affix infrastructure.
- Boss phases: double_strike (second attack when enraged) for Warden/Iron Colossus/Shadow Stalker; ignition (unavoidable fire damage on immune rounds) for Inferno Witch/Abyssal One. isDoubleStrikeRound flag in RoundResult prevents false-positive log entries.
- Study bonuses active: all 4 quality tiers now have mechanical effects — unique auto-identify, rare pulse UI, magic affix count hint, normal socket count hint. Wired in LootScreen and handleVictory.
- Stamina potions: drop from elites (8%), rares (15%), ancient (25%), bosses (30%). ⚡ ST button in CombatScreen. refillPotions includes one Stamina Flask in starting loadout.

decisions.rationale: Every run must be reproducible from a seed. This enables deterministic bun tests, bug reproduction via seed+steps, and future daily-challenge mode. Non-seeded Math.random() would make tests flaky.
alternativesConsidered: xoshiro128 — slightly better distribution but mulberry32 is simpler and sufficient
decisions.recommendation: mulberry32 via makeRng(seed) — zero deps, fast, deterministic. Stack-agnostic: same algorithm works in any JS runtime.

decisions.decision: Engine-first development: all game logic written and bun-tested before any UI screen exists
decisions.rationale: Game systems (loot, combat, grid) are pure data transformations. Keeping them React-free means they can be tested headless, simulated at scale (10k items, 1000 combats), and iterated without a device. UI that depends on untested engine code leads to hard-to-debug emergent bugs.
alternativesConsidered: Build UI first, engine emerges from component state — common in game jams, hard to test
Unity/Godot — built-in test frameworks but abandons RN expertise
decisions.recommendation: Pure TS engine in src/engine/ with bun test. No React imports in engine files. Stack-agnostic: logic survives a React Native → web pivot unchanged.

decisions.decision: Combat as a fast gate (3–6 rounds, numbers-only UI)
decisions.rationale: Playtesting Diablo 2 and similar games shows the dopamine comes from the drop, not the fight. Combat complexity competes for design budget with loot and inventory. Keeping combat to a transaction screen (<5s) lets players do more runs and see more loot.
alternativesConsidered: Turn-based tactics with positioning — fun but requires 10× more design work
Real-time action combat — incompatible with a grid-step model
decisions.recommendation: One action per turn (Attack/Potion/Flee), instant resolution, bold damage numbers, no animations beyond a health bar drain.

decisions.decision: Normal (white) items are valuable via the socket/runeword system
decisions.rationale: D2’s most memorable items are runewords built into white bases. If white items are junk, players stop picking them up and loot excitement drops by 50%. Every white drop with sockets must show a tooltip flag: ‘Runeword candidate (N sockets)’.
alternativesConsidered: Only magic+ items are interesting — simpler but loses D2 DNA
Crafting system — too complex for v1
decisions.recommendation: Normal items: high base stat variance + socket rolls. The tooltip layer makes this visible to players immediately.

decisions.decision: 10×4 Tetris inventory grid with physical item sizes
decisions.rationale: Inventory management as a spatial puzzle is one of D2’s most beloved secondary systems. Charms provide passive bonuses but occupy space, creating a real tradeoff that drives decisions mid-run. A flat list inventory eliminates this tension.
alternativesConsidered: Flat list with item count limit — simpler but no spatial tension
Unlimited stash — removes one of the core decision loops
decisions.recommendation: 10×4 grid, items 1×1 to 2×4, drag-and-drop placement, rotate gesture. Stack-agnostic: same grid logic works in web or native.

successCriteria: Phase 0 (engine): bun test green on all 6 engine modules, sim scripts produce believable loot/encounter distributions. Phase 1 (text UI): fully playable 5-floor run using only text labels and colored boxes — fun validated before any art. Phase 5 (polish): TestFlight build ships with pixel art and sound.
knownLimitations: Requires Mac + Apple Developer account for TestFlight distribution — dev build deferred until Mac access
Pixel art assets not yet sourced — Phase 5 depends on CC0 asset packs (Dungeon Tileset II by 0x72)
No multiplayer or cloud save — single device only in v1
recommendations: React Native + Expo: fastest path given existing RN experience from Recall project
Zustand over Redux — minimal boilerplate, slice pattern maps cleanly to game subsystems
bun test as the test runner — fast, zero config, runs pure TS engine files directly

codeSnippets.label: Seeded RNG — deterministic item generation
Roll loot from an encounter
Headless sim commands (no device needed)
codeSnippets.snippet:
bun run sim:floors 10 150   # 10 floors, 150% magic find — prints all drops
bun run sim:combat 15 ancient  # 1000 combats at floor 15 ancient tier
bun run debug:state            # pretty-print game state snapshot
bun test src/engine            # 94 tests, <200ms

userGuide:
## How to Play
1. Pick a class at the start of each run: **Warrior** (easy), **Rogue** (medium), **Sorcerer** (hard)
2. Use the D-pad (or swipe) to move your character on the dungeon grid
3. Fog of war lifts as you explore — each step reveals the area around you
4. Stepping on an unvisited tile may trigger an encounter (combat, chest, shrine, or empty)
5. In combat: tap Attack, use a skill (mana-gated, class-specific), Use Potion to heal, or Flee
6. After victory, items drop — tap TAKE to add to bag, or leave them
7. Open BAG (bottom of grid screen) to manage your 20-slot inventory
8. Tap TOWN to return to the Town screen — deposit items to the shared stash, equip from stash, and review your character stats. **Note: visiting town from the dungeon has a cost — use it when it matters.**
9. Find the exit staircase (glowing tile) to descend to the next floor
10. Clear floor 10 to advance to the next difficulty tier — the first clear of each tier shows a special '✦ FIRST CLEAR ✦' overlay
11. Death sacrifices one equipped item (quality-weighted) from your gear. Your bag is fully wiped. Stashed items are always safe.

## Grid Controls
- **Swipe / D-pad**: move character
- **Pinch**: zoom grid (3 levels: small / medium / large). Your zoom preference is saved across sessions.
- **Zoom lock button** (🔒): when active, single-finger swipe adjusts zoom instead of moving. Useful for precise navigation on small screens.

## Ghost Echo
From the Graveyard screen, tap a sacrificed item to invoke its Ghost Echo — you gain 25% of its stats for 1 floor. A one-time ambient hint appears on the dungeon grid when your graveyard is non-empty.

## Ascension
On the victory screen, if your graveyard has entries, press **ASCEND** to start a new run with Ghost Echo pre-loaded from your most recent sacrifice. Your ascension count (✦×N) appears on the main menu.

## Pacts (Optional Challenge Modifiers)
Before descending, bind yourself to a Pact — a voluntary curse with a guaranteed reward. Pact persists between runs until you deactivate it.
- **⚔ BLOOD**: +35% damage taken / +50% magic find
- **☠ SUFFERING**: All monsters Cursed (defense halved) / +30% XP
- **◈ FRAILTY**: No potions drop / All drops auto-identified
- **⚡ EXHAUSTION**: Start each floor at 50% stamina / +25% magic find

## Named Variant Enemies
From floor 4+, elite encounters have a 20% chance to be a Named Variant — a fixed-identity enemy with a specific affix combination. Unlike random elites, variants are consistent across runs. Examples: Void Wraith (teleporting+cursed), Ironbone Warrior (extraStrong+aura), Stone Sentinel (aura+extraStrong). See feat-named-variants for the full table.

## Boss Phases
Bosses gain additional mechanics beyond enrage:
- **Double Strike** (Warden, Iron Colossus, Shadow Stalker): Two independent attacks per round when enraged. Block chance applies to each.
- **Ignition** (Inferno Witch, Abyssal One): Unavoidable fire damage on immune rounds, partially reduced by fire resistance.
- **Abyssal One (floor 30)**: Has all four mechanics simultaneously.

## Testing (Dev)
```
bun test src/engine                    # run all engine tests
bun run src/sim/classBalanceSim.ts     # 15-scenario class balance check
bun run src/sim/runSim.ts              # simulate full runs headless
npx tsc --noEmit                       # TypeScript check (0 errors target)
```
componentFile: src/engine/rng.ts, src/engine/grid.ts, src/engine/encounter.ts, src/engine/loot.ts, src/engine/affixes.ts, src/engine/monsters.ts, src/engine/combat.ts, src/engine/inventory.ts, src/sim/runSim.ts, src/sim/lootSim.ts, src/sim/combatSim.ts, src/sim/debugState.ts, src/data/items.ts, src/data/monsters.ts, src/data/runewords.ts

publicInterface.name: generateItem
publicInterface.description: Generate a fully resolved item: quality roll, affix pool pick, socket count, runeword check, display name. Pure function, no side effects.

publicInterface.name: rollLoot
publicInterface.type: function(rng: Rng, encounterType: string, floor: number, magicFind?: number) => Item[]
publicInterface.description: Roll a full loot drop for an encounter type. Returns 0–3 items depending on encounter tier.

publicInterface.name: simulateCombat
publicInterface.type: function(rng: Rng, player: PlayerCombatStats, monster: MonsterInstance) => CombatResult
publicInterface.description: Auto-resolve a full combat. Returns outcome, round log, HP remaining, XP gained. Used by sim scripts.

publicInterface.name: generateFloor
publicInterface.type: function(seed: number, rng: Rng) => { grid, playerStart, exitPos, rooms }
publicInterface.description: BSP dungeon generation. Deterministic by seed. Returns 50×50 tile grid with rooms and corridors carved.

publicInterface.name: makeRng
publicInterface.description: Create a seeded PRNG (mulberry32). All game randomness must flow through this.
